palette_hint: Off-white background #F5F1EB, dark charcoal text #2B2B2B, burnt-sienna accent #C4522A, and muted olive secondary #6B7C4E
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif bold at 1.4–2rem, body in system-ui at 0.9rem regular, giving a rugged-utilitarian field-catalog feel
description: Ironbark Field Supply is an online store selling professional bushcraft, wilderness survival, and land-management tools — axes, saws, cordage, fire-starting kits, and protective clothing — to farmers, rangers, and serious outdoor workers.
constraints: Shop page uses a fixed 260px left sidebar for category/filter controls and a scrollable main product grid (3 columns at ≥1100px, 2 columns at 700–1099px) separated by a 1px solid #D4CCBF divider

Product detail page uses a two-column layout: left column 45% width for a static product image placeholder (#D4CCBF filled rectangle with centered product name text), right column 55% for specs table, pricing, and an Add-to-Cart button in #C4522A

Cart page uses a full-width table (border-collapse, alternating row backgrounds #F5F1EB and #EDE8DF) for line items, with a sticky 280px right-side order-summary panel that stays fixed when the item list scrolls

Account page is two-column: a 220px sticky left nav listing Orders, Addresses, Payment, and Settings sections, with the main content area rendering a dense orders-history table showing order number, date, items count, total, and status badge

All pages share a sticky top header (height 56px, background #2B2B2B, white text) containing the Ironbark logo left-aligned, primary nav links center-aligned, and a cart icon with item-count badge in #C4522A right-aligned

page_specs.shop: Displays 18 product cards across the grid, each card containing a colored-rectangle image placeholder, product name (e.g. 'Gransfors Bruks Small Forest Axe', 'Silky GOMBOY 240 Folding Saw', 'Paracord 550 — 100ft Olive Drab'), price, and an olive-green 'Add to Cart' button. The left sidebar contains collapsible filter groups for Category (Axes & Hatchets, Saws, Cordage, Fire Starting, Clothing), Price Range (checkboxes: Under $25, $25–$75, $75–$150, Over $150), and Brand (Gransfors Bruks, Silky, Bahco, Light My Fire, Carinthia), plus a 'Clear Filters' link in #C4522A. A breadcrumb trail reads 'Home › Shop' and a product count line reads '18 products found' above the grid.
page_specs.product-detail: Showcases the 'Gransfors Bruks Small Forest Axe' at $149.00 USD. The right column contains a two-row specs table (Head Weight: 0.8 kg, Handle Length: 50 cm, Steel Grade: Swedish, Handle Material: American Hickory, Edge Grind: Convex, Includes: Grain-leather sheath) styled with #EDE8DF header cells and #F5F1EB data cells. Below the table are a quantity stepper input, the burnt-sienna Add-to-Cart button (full column width, 48px tall), a 'Save to Wishlist' text link, and a short two-paragraph product description about the axe's Swedish craftsmanship and suitability for limbing and campsite work.
page_specs.cart: Shows three line items in the table: Gransfors Bruks Small Forest Axe ×1 at $149.00, Silky GOMBOY 240 ×2 at $89.00 each, and Paracord 550 100ft ×3 at $12.00 each. Each row has a remove-item '×' link in #C4522A. The sticky right-side summary panel shows Subtotal $351.00, Estimated Shipping $14.95, Tax (8.25%) $28.96, and Order Total $394.91 in bold, followed by a full-width 'Proceed to Checkout' button in #C4522A and a 'Continue Shopping' text link below it. A promo-code input field with an 'Apply' button sits above the totals.
page_specs.account: The sticky left nav highlights 'Orders' as the active section using a #C4522A left border and bold text. The main area shows an 'Order History' heading, then a dense table of 8 past orders with columns: Order #, Date, Items, Total, and Status — statuses styled as small inline badges (Delivered in #6B7C4E, Processing in #C4522A, Cancelled in #888). Above the table, account-holder name 'Dale Murchison' and email 'dale.murchison@ranchmail.net' are displayed in a compact info row with an 'Edit Profile' link.
page_specs.about: A single-column content page (max-width 760px, centered) with three distinct sections separated by 1px #D4CCBF horizontal rules. Section one is a company origin story about Ironbark Field Supply founding in 2011 in Tamworth, NSW by a former land-clearing contractor named Petra Halvorsen. Section two is a three-column grid listing core values — 'Built to Last', 'Expert-Sourced', and 'No Nonsense Returns' — each as a small heading with two sentences of specific detail. Section three contains a simple two-column table of supplier certifications: FSC Certified Timber Handles, ISO 9001 Steel Sourcing, and Australia Post Carbon-Neutral Delivery.
